text: Good morning church, welcome to Sunday service.
It's so good to see all of you here today.
Before we begin, let's just take a moment to greet those around you.
Alright, would you please stand as we open in worship.
Let's sing Amazing Grace together.
Amazing grace how sweet the sound that saved a wretch like me.
I once was lost but now am found was blind but now I see.
Go to the chorus please.
Through many dangers toils and snares I have already come.
Beautiful. Now let's stand as we sing How Great Thou Art.
Oh Lord my God when I in awesome wonder consider all the worlds thy hands have made.
Then sings my soul my Saviour God to thee how great thou art how great thou art.
Next slide please.
When Christ shall come with shout of acclamation and take me home what joy shall fill my heart.
Verse two
Let's now bring it down and sing Holy Holy Holy.
Holy holy holy Lord God almighty early in the morning our song shall rise to thee.
Holy holy holy merciful and mighty God in three persons blessed trinity.
Church please be seated.
We're going to fade to a video from our missions team in Kenya.
Amen. God is doing amazing things around the world.
A quick announcement — youth camp registration closes this Wednesday.
Sign-up sheets are at the welcome desk in the foyer.
And a second announcement, we need volunteers for next Saturday's community outreach.
Speak to Pastor James if you can help.
Alright, please open your Bibles with me to John chapter three verse sixteen.
For God so loved the world that he gave his only begotten Son.
That whosoever believeth in him should not perish but have everlasting life.
This is the gospel in one verse church.
Turn with me now to Romans eight verse twenty eight.
And we know that all things work together for good to them that love God.
To them who are the called according to his purpose.
Amen. Let's also read from Psalm 91.
He that dwelleth in the secret place of the most High shall abide under the shadow of the Almighty.
I will say of the Lord he is my refuge and my fortress my God in him will I trust.
Now look at Genesis 1 from verse 1 to 3.
In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth.
And the earth was without form and void and darkness was upon the face of the deep.
And God said let there be light and there was light.
Let's continue in Ephesians 2:8-9.
For by grace are ye saved through faith and that not of yourselves it is the gift of God.
Not of works lest any man should boast.
Grace church. Grace. It's amazing grace as we sang earlier.
Turn to Philippians four verse thirteen.
I can do all things through Christ which strengtheneth me.
Now flip over to Second Timothy 1:7.
For God hath not given us the spirit of fear but of power and of love and of a sound mind.
And First Corinthians thirteen verse four says love is patient love is kind.
It does not envy it does not boast it is not proud.
Now hear Matthew 6:33.
But seek ye first the kingdom of God and his righteousness and all these things shall be added unto you.
One more — Isaiah 40:31.
But they that wait upon the Lord shall renew their strength.
They shall mount up with wings as eagles.
They shall run and not be weary they shall walk and not faint.
Church, God's word is alive.
Now hear Proverbs three verses five and six.
Trust in the Lord with all thine heart and lean not unto thine own understanding.
In all thy ways acknowledge him and he shall direct thy paths.
So don't just say I trust God, actually trust him.
The Lord will make a way somehow.
That was actually a paraphrase not a direct quote.
Let me share a story from my week that ties into this.
I was reading and thinking about grace, faith, and forgiveness.
And the Spirit reminded me of something my grandmother used to say.
She'd say only Jesus can heal a broken heart, not amazing grace by itself.
That is deep. Let that sink in.
Alright — for our next scripture, let's look at Hebrews 11 verse 1.
Now faith is the substance of things hoped for the evidence of things not seen.
And we know from Romans ten seventeen that faith comes by hearing the word of God.
So keep reading, keep hearing, keep believing.
Look at James chapter 1 verse 22.
Be ye doers of the word and not hearers only deceiving your own selves.
Church, hearing without doing is deception.
Let me pause and pray for anyone hurting today.
Father, we thank you. We honor you.
You are worthy of all our praise.
Heal every wounded heart in this room today, in Jesus' name.
Amen. Amen.
Musicians, come back up. Let's sing It Is Well With My Soul.

expected.song: It Is Well With My Soul

text: When peace like a river attendeth my way when sorrows like sea billows roll.

expected.song: It Is Well With My Soul

text: Whatever my lot thou hast taught me to say it is well it is well with my soul.

expected.song: It Is Well With My Soul

text: The chorus says it is well with my soul.

expected.song: It Is Well With My Soul

text: Verse three please.
My sin oh the bliss of this glorious thought my sin not in part but the whole.

expected.song: It Is Well With My Soul

text: Is nailed to the cross and I bear it no more praise the Lord praise the Lord O my soul.

expected.song: It Is Well With My Soul

text: Now let's sing Great Is Thy Faithfulness.
Great is thy faithfulness O God my Father there is no shadow of turning with thee.
Thou changest not thy compassions they fail not as thou hast been thou forever wilt be.
Great is thy faithfulness great is thy faithfulness morning by morning new mercies I see.
Go to the chorus.
All I have needed thy hand hath provided great is thy faithfulness Lord unto me.
Amen, amen. What a mighty God.
As we close let's remember Matthew 28 verses 19 and 20.
Go ye therefore and teach all nations baptizing them.
Teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I have commanded you.
And lo I am with you alway even unto the end of the world amen.
That's our great commission church.
Let's close in worship — Come Thou Fount of Every Blessing.

expected.song: Come Thou Fount of Every Blessing

text: Come thou fount of every blessing tune my heart to sing thy grace.

expected.song: Come Thou Fount of Every Blessing

text: Streams of mercy never ceasing call for songs of loudest praise.

expected.song: Come Thou Fount of Every Blessing

text: Teach me some melodious sonnet sung by flaming tongues above.

expected.song: Come Thou Fount of Every Blessing

text: Prone to wander Lord I feel it prone to leave the God I love.

expected.song: Come Thou Fount of Every Blessing

text: Here's my heart Lord take and seal it seal it for thy courts above.

expected.song: Come Thou Fount of Every Blessing

text: Next slide please.
One more time church, sing it out.
Alright church, receive the benediction.
May the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
The love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with you always.
See Numbers 6 verses 24 to 26 for the priestly blessing.
The Lord bless thee and keep thee.
The Lord make his face shine upon thee and be gracious unto thee.
The Lord lift up his countenance upon thee and give thee peace.
Amen. God bless you church, see you next week.
Safe travels home everyone.
